expectations.text: Output is primarily written in Bahasa Indonesia
expectations.evidence: The entire caption is written in Bahasa Indonesia: 'Kulit kusam?', 'kamu cuma butuh 14 hari buat glow up', 'mencerahkan, meratakan warna kulit', etc. Only minor English loanwords common in Indonesian social media ('girl', 'glow up', 'filter') appear.

expectations.text: Contains at least 2 distinct copy variations
expectations.evidence: Only one caption is provided. There is no second variation or alternative copy. The output contains a single 'Caption:' section with no additional versions.
Brand name appears in the title ('GlowNesia Serum Vitamin C 15%'), in the caption body ('Kenalan sama GlowNesia Vitamin C Serum 15%'), and in hashtags ('#GlowNesia').

expectations.text: Highlights a specific product benefit related to brightening/mencerahkan (not just listing the vitamin C feature)
expectations.evidence: The caption highlights brightening as a benefit: 'bekerja mencerahkan, meratakan warna kulit, dan bikin wajahmu glowing dari dalam' and 'Kulit cerah, sehat, dan siap tanpa filter.' This goes beyond just mentioning vitamin C.
Two CTAs present: 'Jangan sampai nyesel nggak checkout hari ini, babe' and 'Tap link di bio sekarang'.

expectations.text: Uses casual/informal register appropriate for Gen Z audience (no 'Anda', uses conversational tone)
expectations.evidence: Tone is casual throughout: uses 'kamu', 'Girl', 'babe', 'nggak', 'glow up', 'nolak', 'nyesel'. No formal 'Anda' appears. Register matches Gen Z social media language.

expectations.text: Mentions the price point (89 ribu or 89.000 or 89rb or equivalent)
Includes urgency element tied to the promo (hari ini/sekarang/terbatas or similar)
expectations.evidence: Multiple urgency elements: 'HARI INI AJA!' (all caps for emphasis), 'Jangan sampai nyesel nggak checkout hari ini', and 'Stok terbatas!'.

claims.evidence: Matches the prompt input which specified 'serum vitamin C 15%'. Caption correctly states 'Vitamin C Serum 15%'.
Matches the prompt: 'mencerahkan wajah dalam 14 hari'. Caption says 'kamu cuma butuh 14 hari buat glow up'.

claims.claim: Free shipping promo is today only
claims.evidence: Matches the prompt: 'promo gratis ongkir hari ini aja'. Caption states 'GRATIS ONGKIR — HARI INI AJA!'.

suggestions.assertion: Contains at least 2 distinct copy variations
suggestions.reason: This is the key differentiator assertion — it tests whether the agent provides multiple options as a professional copywriter would. It correctly caught that the baseline output only produced one version. Good assertion.

No assertion checks for appropriate caption length for Instagram (typically under 2200 characters). The output is well within limits, but a length assertion could catch edge cases where the agent produces excessively long or short copy.
No assertion checks whether hashtags are included. For an Instagram caption, hashtags are a standard deliverable. The output includes them, but this goes unverified by the current assertion set.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-targeted and discriminating. The 'at least 2 variations' assertion is especially useful for distinguishing skilled copywriting output from a generic single-caption response. Consider adding a hashtag presence check since it's standard for Instagram content.
